title: US Defence Secretary Hegseth warns of 'invasion' on European shores in D-Day speech
content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a D-Day anniversary speech in Normandy to warn European leaders about migration, saying that “boats and men” arriving on southern European shores posed a challenge that governments could no longer ignore.

title: US downs two Iranian drones threatening Strait of Hormuz traffic
content: US forces intercepted two Iranian attack drones near the Strait of Hormuz, a day after downing four others that Washington said threatened maritime traffic in the strategic waterway.
snippet: US forces intercepted two Iranian attack drones near the Strait of Hormuz, a day after downing four others that Washington said threatened maritime traffic in the strategic waterway.

title: US defence chief uses D-Day speech to warn Europe on migration ‘invasion’
content: Pete Hegseth drew controversy for saying, "Boats and men arrive. When will European capitals do something about that invasion? Or is it too late?"
snippet: Pete Hegseth drew controversy for saying, "Boats and men arrive. When will European capitals do something about that invasion? Or is it too late?"

title: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Lebanon as Middle East tensions escalate
content: At least five people were killed in two separate Israeli strikes in Lebanon, while 22 others were wounded, including three children, according to Lebanese authorities. The attacks came as the Iran conflict entered its 100th day.

title: D-Day 82nd anniversary honoured in France
content: World leaders and the last remaining veterans gathered in Normandy on Saturday to mark the 82nd anniversary of the D-Day landings. Commemorations in Langrune-sur-Mer and Colleville-sur-Mer paid tribute to the 160,000 Allied troops who launched the liberation of Europe in 1944.

title: Pope Leo XIV calls for national reconciliation on his first day in Spain
content: Pope Leo XIV set foot on Spanish soil on Saturday 6 June, ending a fifteen‑year papal absence. Over 130,000 people greeted him in Madrid, where he warned against ready‑made ideologies and called for reconciliation.

title: Polish and Ukrainian officials meet in Warsaw as tensions mount over 'UPA' military unit naming row
content: The Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA) is believed by Ukrainians to be a key pillar behind their independence, while Warsaw accuses it of carrying out "genocide" against Poles between 1943-45.

title: Trump 'still disappointed' with NATO over Iran, warns US Ambassador
content: US Ambassador to the EU Andrew Pudzer says NATO allies made a 'big mistake' not assisting the US in the war in Iran. In an interview with Euronews, he said Europeans had an obligation to help, given America's history of defending the European continent.

title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says Russia could attack NATO within four years

title: Albania's Edi Rama tells Euronews there is 'no alternative' to EU accession

Countries like France are pushing for a tougher stance against

title: Europe Today: Costa speaks exclusively to Euronews as EU-Western Balkans summit underway
content: A high-stakes EU-Western Balkans summit is underway in Montenegro, with enlargement in the spotlight and France and Germany pushing for a more gradual path to EU integration. Our EU Editor Maria Tadeo is on the ground and speaks exclusively to European Council President António Costa.
